content.problemStatement:
Chef has two integers XXX and YYY. Chef wants to perform some operations to make XXX and YYY equal. In one operation, Chef can either:


set X:=X+1X := X + 1X:=X+1
or set Y:=Y+2Y := Y + 2Y:=Y+2
Find the minimum number of operations required to make XXX and YYY equal.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space separated integers XXX and YYY.


Output Format
For each test case, print the minimum number of operations required to make XXX and YYY equal.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Let (x,y)(x, y)(x,y) denote the values of XXX and YYY respectively.
Test case 111: Only one operation is required: (3,4)→X:=X+1(4,4)(3, 4) \xrightarrow{X := X + 1}{} (4, 4)(3,4)X:=X+1​(4,4)
Test case 222: No operations are required since XXX and YYY are already equal.
Test case 333: Two operations are required: (7,3)→Y:=Y+2(7,5)→Y:=Y+2(7,7)(7, 3) \xrightarrow{Y := Y + 2}{} (7, 5) \xrightarrow{Y := Y + 2}{} (7, 7)(7,3)Y:=Y+2​(7,5)Y:=Y+2​(7,7)
Test case 444: Three operations are required. One such sequence of operations is: (5,2)→Y:=Y+2(5,4)→X:=X+1(6,4)→Y:=Y+2(6,6)(5, 2) \xrightarrow{Y := Y + 2}{} (5, 4) \xrightarrow{X := X + 1}{} (6, 4) \xrightarrow{Y := Y + 2}{} (6, 6)(5,2)Y:=Y+2​(5,4)X:=X+1​(6,4)Y:=Y+2​(6,6)
